source.cookbook: Black Rican Vegan   Fire Plant Based Recipes from
source.author: Various

ingredients.item:
COMPRA LIST
1 large yuca
4 green bananas
Salt
1 (14-oz [400-g]) can hearts of palm, drained
1 tbsp (3 g) nori flakes
1 small white onion, sliced
½ cup (50 g) green olives
1 avocado, pitted, peeled and diced
3 tbsp (45 ml) olive oil
1 tsp red wine vinegar
1½ tsp (4.5 g) Adobo
1½ tsp (4.5 g) garlic powder
½ tsp freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.original:
COMPRA LIST
1 large yuca
4 green bananas
Salt
1 (14-oz [400-g]) can hearts of palm, drained
1 tbsp (3 g) nori flakes
1 small white onion, sliced
½ cup (50 g) green olives
1 avocado, pitted, peeled and diced
3 tbsp (45 ml) olive oil
1 tsp red wine vinegar
1½ tsp (4.5 g) Adobo
1½ tsp (4.5 g) garlic powder
½ tsp freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.item:
Peel your yuca and green bananas and cut them into ½-inch (1.3-cm) pieces.
In a large pot, bring water to a boil. Once boiling, add your yuca and green banana with about 1 tablespoon (18 g) of salt and cook for 15 minutes, or until tender. Drain and let cool.
On a cutting board, roughly chop the hearts of palm into bacalao-like pieces (small and oval, almost like anchovies). They don’t have to look perfect! Season with a pinch of salt and the nori flakes to give them a hint of sea flavor that mimics salt fish.

Once the yuca and banana are cool, transfer them to a bowl and add the hearts of palm, onion, olives, avocado, olive oil, red wine vinegar, adobo, garlic powder and pepper, and mix.

The gazpacho is complete, and you can eat it with white rice or by itself.
